title: Uzbekistan national football team names expanded squad for 2026 FIFA World Cup
original_text:
Head coach of the Uzbekistan national football team Fabio Cannavaro named an expanded 30-player squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, the Uzbekistan Football Association said .

According to the association, the team will depart for the United States on 26 May, flying from Tashkent to New York for a training camp.

The final World Cup squad will include 26 players, meaning the coaching staff will have to remove four players from the current list before the tournament begins.

First vice president of the Uzbekistan Football Association Ravshan Irmatov previously explained that the additional players were included as cover in case of injuries or unexpected situations during preparations.

Goalkeepers: Utkir Yusupov (Navbahor), Botirali Ergashev (Neftchi), Abduvohid Nematov (Nasaf). Advertising on Gazeta

Defenders: Rustamjon Ashurmatov (Esteghlal, Iran), Umrbek Eshmurodov (Nasaf), Avazbek Ulmasaliev (AGMK), Abduqodir Husanov (Manchester City, England), Abdulla Abdullaev (Dibba, UAE), Jakhongir Urozov (Dinamo), Khojiakbar Alijonov (Pakhtakor), Behruz Karimov (Surkhon), Ruslanbek Jiyanov (Navbahor), Farrukh Sayfiev (Neftchi), Sherzod Nasrullaev (Pakhtakor), Umirali Rakhmonaliev (Sabah, Azerbaijan).

Midfielders: Azizjon Ganiev (Al Bataeh, UAE), Akmal Mozgovoy (Pakhtakor), Sherzod Esanov (Bukhara), Otabek Shukurov (Baniyas, UAE), Odiljon Khamrobekov (Tractor, Iran), Dostonbek Khamdamov (Pakhtakor), Jamshid Iskanderov (Neftchi), Jasur Jaloliddinov (Sogdiana).

Forwards: Abbosbek Fayzullaev (Istanbul Başakşehir, Turkey), Oston Urunov (Persepolis, Iran), Jaloliddin Masharipov (Esteghlal, Iran), Azizbek Amonov (Dinamo), Eldor Shomurodov (Istanbul Başakşehir, Turkey), Igor Sergeev (Persepolis, Iran), Sherzod Temirov (Erbil, Iraq).

The following players who participated in the training camp will not travel to the United States : Vladimir Nazarov, Muhammadrasul Abdumajidov, Diyor Ortikboev, Kuvondyk Ruzyev, Nodir Abdurazzoqov, Alisher Odilov, Sardor Bakhromov, Khusayin Norchaev, Muhammadqodir Hamraliev.

One player, Ibrokhimkhalil Yuldoshev, will miss the tournament due to injury, while Azizbek Turgunboev was not included in the list.

As part of their preparation, Uzbekistan will play friendly matches against Canada on 1 June and the Netherlands on 8 June.

Uzbekistan will feature at the 2026 World Cup for the first time in history. The team is drawn in Group K, where they will face Colombia, Portugal and the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

The national team will begin their campaign on 18 June against Colombia in Mexico City, before facing Portugal on 23 June in Houston and finishing the group stage against DR Congo on 28 June in Atlanta.
